reasoning:
For 2026-01-25: Wikipedia's FIDE rankings article reflects standard FIDE classical rating updates, which are strictly published on the 1st of every month. The January list will remain active on January 25, identically matching the rating shown on January 18 (2751). Since an identical rating represents a 0% change, resolving 'at least 1% higher' is definitively false.

For 2026-02-17: This will fetch the February 1, 2026 standard rating list. A 1% increase on Nodirbek Abdusattorov's 2751 base rating implies he needs an increment of roughly 28 points, requiring him to reach 2779. Gaining +28 points in a single month for a >2750 super-grandmaster demands a virtually unprecedented performance (approximately +6 to +8 score in a 13-round elite tournament). He is currently playing in the Tata Steel Masters (January 2026), but he played a 9-move draw in the very first round against Yagiz Erdogmus. Drawing one of his weakest-rated opponents immediately incurs a rating deduction, making mathematical realization of a +28 point requirement across the remaining 12 rounds incredibly unlikely. Assigned lowest possible probabilities (bounded at 0.05/0.06 to respect system constraints, with slight widening for the further resolution date).
